One Link
How it works Features Security Share One Get One Link
About

Connection that belongs to you.

Every message, every file, every conversation, yours. Held by you, sent by you, opened by the people you choose. One Link is the network as it was always meant to be: free, direct, and built to bring us closer.

What we are

One Link is a free, private network for messages, files, and devices. It works on phones, laptops, tablets, and small embedded boards. It works on a plane, on a boat, in a basement with no internet. It works when the relay we run goes dark, because anyone can run a relay.

Built so a single person, a family, a school, a movement, or a whole town can talk and share without anyone in the middle. Built so a journalist can hand documents to a source without leaving a trace. Built so a kid can send a 50GB game to their friend without uploading it to anyone first.

What we stand for.

Common infrastructure for human connection. Free, in every sense of the word. Open, in every line. Yours, by the architecture itself. We chose AGPL on purpose so the source stays in the open forever; any fork built on it stays open too. The work belongs to whoever wants to use it, run it, share it, improve it.

Every line ships verifiable. Every release is signed. Your One Link works on its own once installed, because it was built to outlast the people who started it. If we step away tomorrow, the network keeps running on the relays you and others are already operating. That is the design.

Who is behind it

Anyone willing to read the code, run a relay, file a bug, or argue with a design decision. There is no company. There are no founders to put on a slide. The work belongs to whoever picks it up. The license (AGPL) makes that promise enforceable.

The protocol grew out of a broader research effort on coherence-field topology, post-quantum cryptography, field-witness binding, and formal verification. One Link is the most public, most useful expression of that work. The lab and the math behind it are open too. We are one.

The covenant

Free forever. Open forever. Your data is yours. Your identity is yours. Your conversations live between you and the people you chose. Every release is signed in a sealed environment so anyone can verify what they're running matches what was built. Every promise here is locked into the architecture, not into our intentions.

That is the whole promise. Held by the math and the license, so it stays true even when the people change.

Get One Link → Donate
One Link

A free, private network for messages, files, and devices. Owned by no one. Belongs to everyone.

Built in the open. AGPL-3.0. No tracking, no analytics, no cookies. we are one
